expectations.text: Output contains a properly structured search matrix with Context, Tier 1, Tier 2, and Tier 3 sections
expectations.evidence: Output is a phased research plan with 6 phases, not a tiered search matrix

expectations.text: Matrix uses at least 3 different engine categories (e.g., MDN, GitHub, Google, caniuse)
expectations.evidence: No engine categories specified. Plan mentions caniuse by name in search terms but does not structure queries by engine

expectations.text: Search queries use engine-specific operators such as language:, stars:>50, or site: filters
expectations.evidence: No engine-specific operators. All search terms are plain text phrases

expectations.text: Tier 2 queries broaden scope compared to Tier 1, and Tier 3 explores alternative or lateral sources
expectations.evidence: No tier-based escalation. Phases are organized by topic (specs, browsers, servers, benchmarks, migration)

expectations.text: Each row includes specific acceptance criteria for evaluating search results
expectations.evidence: Phase 2 mentions 'expected output' of a support table, but no per-query acceptance criteria with thresholds

expectations.text: Output includes a Runtime Recovery section with fallback strategies
expectations.evidence: No Runtime Recovery section. Risks section mentions general adoption concerns but not search fallbacks

expectations.text: Output includes a Grading Summary section with per-tier pass/fail thresholds
At least one query targets browser compatibility or feature detection data
expectations.evidence: Phase 2 is dedicated to browser support with search terms like 'WebTransport browser support' and 'WebTransport caniuse'

expectations.text: At least one query targets latency or throughput benchmarks comparing WebTransport and WebSocket
expectations.evidence: Phase 4 includes 'WebTransport vs WebSocket benchmark' and lists specific metrics (p50, p99, throughput)

expectations.text: Matrix includes queries for server-side WebTransport implementations in at least 2 languages
expectations.evidence: Phase 3 lists search terms for Go, Rust, and Node.js server implementations
